Course Number and Title:
402-0368-07L  
       Lecture Series: Space Research and Exploration
Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: Lecture Series about selected topics of space research and exploration consisting of individual talks given by different leading experts from academia and industry.

Comment 2: Die Vorlesung findet im HPH G 1 mit Videoüebtragung ins HPV G 5.
Short Description: One-semester introduction to the fundamentals and thinking of physics: electricity and magnetism, electromagnetic waves, waves and the Doppler effect. In-depth study of selected topics in modern physics of great technological or industrial importance.

Comment 2: Do 14-16 im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1.
Short Description: This lecture is the continuation of Linear Algebra I. Linear algebra is an indispensable tool in engineering mathematics. The lecture offers an introduction to the theory with numerous applications. The concepts learned are consolidated in the accompanying exercises.

Comment 2: Vorlesung Mo 12-14 im HG F 7 mit Videoübertragung ins HG F 5 und Fri 10-12 im ETA F 5.
Short Description: The lecture "Materials and Manufacturing" teaches students the basics for systematically selecting materials for applications in mechanical engineering.

Comment 2: The Wednesday lectures are held in ML D 28 with video transmission to ML E 12.The course starts in the first week of the semester.
Short Description: Analysis and synthesis for linear time-invariant control systems with one input and one output signal (SISO). State-space models, time response, stability conditions. Transfer functions and frequency response. Stability analysis under feedback: Root Locus, Bode plots, Nyquist condition. Feedback control synthesis: time- and frequency-domain specifications, PID lead/lag compensation, loop shaping.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: Signals arise in most engineering applications. They contain information about the behavior of physical systems. Systems respond to signals and produce other signals. In this course, we explore how signals can be represented and manipulated, and their effects on systems. We further explore how we can discover basic system properties by exciting a system with various types of signals.

Comment 2: The course starts in the second week.
Short Description: Physical principles of nuclear fission and chain reaction, thermal design, construction, function and operation of nuclear reactors and nuclear power plants, light water reactors and other reactor types, conversion and brewing

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: We will provide an overview on how to kinematically and dynamically model typical robotic systems such as robot arms, legged robots, rotary wing systems, or fixed wing.
Restrictions: : Für Fachstudierende und Hörer/-innen ist eine Spezialbewilligung der Dozierenden notwendig

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: Introduction to the fundamentals of heat transfer and technical applications of engineering thermodynamics. Extension of thermodynamic fundamentals taught in Thermodynamics I and II.

Comment 1: The course (both lecture and exercise) consists of two parts: part 1 will be in English and part 2 in German.
Comment 2: Vorlesung Mo 14-16 im ETA F 5, Di 12-14  im ETA F 5 und Mi 12-14 im HG E 7 mit Videoübertragung ins HG E 3.
Short Description: An introduction to the physical and mathematical foundations of fluid dynamics is given. Topics include dimensional analysis, integral and differential conservation laws, inviscid and viscous flows, Navier-Stokes equations, boundary layers, turbulence, potential flows, unsteady flows, aerodynamic concepts, vorticity dynamics, compressible flows.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: The course provides a broad introduction to modern techniques in scientific computing, useful for tasks ranging from data analysis to engineering computations. For each topic, a solid theoretical foundation is combined with extensive exposure to practical examples and coding exercises.

Comment 2: Lectures in HG F7 with video transmission to HG F5.
Short Description: Introduction to partial differential equations. Differential equations which are important in applications are classified and solved. Elliptic, parabolic and hyperbolic differential equations are treated. The following mathematical tools are introduced: Laplace transforms, Fourier series, separation of variables, methods of characteristics.

Comment 2: Unterricht im HG F 7 mit Videoübertragung ins HG F 5.
Short Description: Introduction to thermodynamics of cycles as well as reactive systems

Comment 2: Zusätzlich wird das Study Center angeboten: Donnerstags 18-20 ab der 3. Semesterwoche im HG F1, wo die Möglichkeit des betreuten Lernens angeboten wird. Im Study Center können Studierende Vorlesungsstoff vor- oder nachbereiten und Übungen lösen.
Short Description: Computer Science II lays the foundation for understanding, designing and analyzing algorithms and data structures, and provides an overview of various programming concepts such as functional programming and static and dynamically typed programming languages.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: The students go through a product development process from the initial idea to the functional product. The participants learn to work on a complex development task in a team (5 people), to structure a given problem and to generate and evaluate ideas as well as to design and realize the product with subsequent verification.

Comment 2: Vorlesung im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1
Short Description: Introduction to the mathematical foundations of engineering as far as they relate to differential and integral calculus.

Course Number and Title:
151-0662-00L  
       Programming for Robotics - Introduction to ROS
Comment 1: This course targets senior Bachelor students as well as Master students focusing on Robotics, Systems, and Control. Priority is given to people conducting a project work in the field.
Comment 2: Block course during the first two weeks of the semester.
Short Description: This course gives an introduction to the Robot Operating System (ROS) including many of the available tools that are commonly used in robotics. With the help of different examples, the course should provide a good starting point for students to work with robots. They learn how to create software including simulation, to interface sensors and actuators, and to integrate control algorithms.

Lecturers:
M. Dunn Cavelty, 
 F. J. Egloff
Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: The lecture provides an introduction to the global politics of cyber security. The focus is on the strategic use of cyberspace by state and non-state actors (threats) and different responses to these new challenges (countermeasures).

Comment 2: Vorlesung im ML D 28 mit Videoübertragung ins ML E 12.
Short Description: Introduction to the theory and fundamentals of technical thermodynamics.

Comment 1: Note: The previous course title until FS23 "Introduction to Quantum Mechanics for Engineers"
Comment 2: Vorlesung im HG E 7 mit Videoübertragung ins HG E 3.
Short Description: This course provides fundamental knowledge in the principles of quantum mechanics and connects it to applications in engineering.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: The Bachelor's thesis is completed in the 6th semester. It corresponds to 480 hours and can be completed part-time or full-time.
Restrictions: : Für Fachstudierende und Hörer/-innen ist eine Spezialbewilligung der Dozierenden notwendig

Comment 2: The exercises will start in the 2nd week of the Semester.
Short Description: Most problems in engineering are non-linear in nature. The nonlinearities are mainly caused by nonlinear material behavior, contact conditions and structural instabilities. This lecture teaches the theoretical principles of non-linear finite element methods for solving such problems.

Comment 2: Gruppeneinteilung erfolgt über myStudies.Vorlesung Mo 15-16 im HG E7 mit Videoübertragung ins HG E5Die Lehrveranstaltung (Vorlesung und Übung) beginnt in der 2. Semesterwoche.
Short Description: This course provides an introduction to engineering design. Through hands-on, practice-oriented exercises, students experience the fundamentals of design concept generation and selecting materials. They create 3D models in CAD for their own customized design and fabricate them using 3D printing. Three case studies in healthcare, mobility and sustainable materials will be explored.
Restrictions: : Für Fachstudierende und Hörer/-innen ist eine Spezialbewilligung der Dozierenden notwendig

Comment 2: Donnerstags 18-20 ab der 3. Semesterwoche im HG F 7 wo die Möglichkeit des betreuten Lernens angeboten wird. Im Study Center können Studierende Vorlesungsstoff vor- oder nachbereiten und Übungen lösen.
Short Description: This is a general chemistry course aimed at first-year bachelor students in the Department of Mechanical and Process Engineering.

Course Number and Title:
151-0306-00L  
       Visualization, Simulation and Interaction - Virtual Reality I
Comment 2: Start in the second week of the Semester.
Short Description: Virtual reality technology. Human factors, creation of virtual worlds, lighting models, display and sound systems, tracking, haptic/tactile interaction, motion platforms, virtual prototypes, data exchange, complete VR systems, augmented reality; collaboration systems; VR and design; implementation of VR in industry; human computer interfaces (HCI).

Lecturers:
S. Andraszewicz, 
 C. Hölscher, 
 A. C. Roberts
Comment 2: The lecturers will communicate the exact lesson times of ONLINE courses.
Short Description: In this seminar, students will study cognitive processes, behaviour and the underlying biological response to financial decisions. Research methods such as asset market experiments, lottery games, risk preference assessment, psychometrics, neuroimaging and psychophysiology of decision processes will be discussed. Financial bubbles and crashes will be the core interest.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: Lagrangian dynamics - Principle of virtual work and virtual power - holonomic and non holonomic contraints - 3D rigid body dynamics  - equilibrium - linearization - stability - vibrations - frequency response

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: The course “Coaching Students” enhance Student Teaching Assistants (Student TAs) in their role as student coaches to develop basic knowledge about coaching methodology and the mindset of a coach.

Comment 1: Note: The previous course title until FS23 "Computational Mechanics I: Intro to FEA"
Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: We discuss numerical methods for solving initial boundary value problems in solid mechanics (static/dynamic elastic problems of solids and structures, thermal problems). Focus is on finite differences and on the finite element method, its theoretical foundation, the choices made when using it, its application for solving problems of engineering interest, and the interpretation of results.

Comment 2: Die Übungen zu "Elektrotechnik I" beginnen in der 2. Semesterwoche.Zusätzlich wird das Study Center angeboten: Mittwochs 18:00 - 20:00 Uhr ab der 3. Semesterwoche im HG F 5 wo die Möglichkeit des betreuten Lernens angeboten wird. Im Study Center können Studierende Vorlesungsstoff vor- oder nachbereiten und Übungen lösen.
Short Description: Basic lecture in the field of electrical engineering with the following topics: Concepts of voltage and current; analysis of direct and alternating current networks; series and parallel circuits of (complex) resistance networks; Kirchhoff's laws and other network theorems; transient processes; fundamentals of electric and magnetic fields;

Lecturers:
P. Cheridito, 
 C. Cotrini Jimenez, 
 A. Streich
Comment 2: Findet am Freitag Nachmittag im HG F 1 mit Videoübertragung ins HG F 3 statt.Zusätzlich wird das Study Center angeboten: Mittwochs 18:15-20:00 ab der 3. Semesterwoche im ETA F 5, wo die Möglichkeit des betreuten Lernens angeboten wird. Im Study Center können Studierende Vorlesungsstoff vor- oder nachbereiten und Übungen lösen.
Short Description: This is an introduction to probability, statistics, and machine learning for students of mechanical engineering. We cover the fundamental concepts from probability theory, statistics and machine learning, with a focus on applications for mechanical engineering.

Comment 2: Gruppeneinteilung erfolgt über myStudies.Vorlesung im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1.
Short Description: Linear algebra is an indispensable tool in engineering mathematics. The lecture offers an introduction to the theory with numerous applications. The concepts learned are consolidated in the accompanying exercises. The lecture is continued as Linear Algebra II.

Comment 2: Vorlesung im HG F 1 mit Videoübertragung ins HG F3.
Short Description: The lecture teaches the fundamentals of the development and design of technical systems. The operating principle, functions, areas of application and calculation principles are taught for the most important machine elements. Building on this, the fundamentals of the development, design and manufacture of products are dealt with using practical case studies.

Comment 1: Number of participants limited to 12.- Priority is given to students in the Design, Mechanics and Manufacturing Focus Specialization in the Mechanical Engineering BSc - To apply, please send the following information to jchapuis@ethz.ch by 31.08.2024: Letter of Motivation (one page) , CV, Transcript of Records- Permission from lecturers required for all students.
Comment 2:
Bewilligung der Dozierenden für alle Studierenden notwendig. 
  The course takes place on Wednesdays at 14-18h starting in the second week of the semester.
Short Description: This course introduces students to engineering design and fabrication by building their own skis or snowboard. Theoretical and applied engineering design skills like CAD, analysis and engineering of mechanical properties, 3D printing, laser cutting and practical handcrafting skills are acquired in the course.
Restrictions: : Für Fachstudierende und Hörer/-innen ist eine Spezialbewilligung der Dozierenden notwendigBewilligung der Dozierenden für alle Studierenden notwendig

Comment 1: Note: the previous course title until HS23 "Leichtbau" in German.
Comment 2: The course starts in the 2nd semester week.
Short Description: The elective course Lightweight includes numerical methods for the analysis of the load carrying and failure behavior of lightweight structures, as well as construction methods and design principles for lightweight design.

Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: Teams of 2 - 3 students must design, dimension and manufacture a structure that is as light as possible and meets the specified requirements. An airplane wing spar prototype and later a second improved spar are tested and evaluated with regard to design and structural-mechanical aspects.

Course Number and Title:
151-0221-00L  
       Introduction to Modeling and Optimization of Sustainable Energy Systems
Lecturers:
G. Sansavini, 
 A. Bardow, 
 S. Moret
Comment 2: N/A
Short Description: This course introduces the fundamentals of energy system modeling for the analysis and the optimization of the energy system design and operations.

Comment 2: Montag und Mittwoch im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF C 1.Freitag im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1.
Short Description: Differential and integral calculus of functions of one and several variables; vector analysis; ordinary differential equations of first and higher order, systems of differential equations; power series. In each section, a large number of application examples from mechanics, physics and other areas of engineering studies.

Comment 2: The Monday lectures are held in HG F 7 with video transmission to HG F 5 (except on Monday 16.10.2023 at 16:15-18:00 in HG E 7 with video transmission to HG E 5)The Wednesday lectures are held in ML D 28 (ETH Zentrum at 14:15 - 16:00) with video transmission to HCI G 7  (ETH Hönggerberg at 14:15 - 16:00). The lectures will start on Wednesday 20.09.2023.
Short Description: Dynamics of particles, rigid bodies, and deformable bodies: Motion of a single particle, motion of systems of particles, 2D and 3D motion of rigid bodies, vibrations, waves.

Comment 2: Vorlesung: Mo und Di im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1In der ersten Semesterwoche fällt das Kolloquium aus und wird durch eine Doppelstunde Vorlesung Di 14-16 ersetzt.
Short Description: Fundamentals: Position of a material point; velocity; kinematics of rigid bodies; forces, reaction principle; power statics: force groups and moments; principle of virtual powers, rest position and equilibrium, main theorem of statics; bearing bonds and bearing forces; parallel forces and center of gravity; statics of systems; trusses; friction; rope statics; stress in beam girders.

Comment 2: Die Übungen beginnen in der zweiten Semesterwoche.Zusätzlich wird das Study Center angeboten: Mittwochs 18-20 ab der 3. Semesterwoche im HG F 1, wo die Möglichkeit des betreuten Lernens angeboten wird. Im Study Center können Studierende Vorlesungsstoff vor- oder nachbereiten und Übungen lösen.
Short Description: The lecture offers an introduction to programming with a focus on systematic algorithmic problem solving. The teaching language is C++. No programming experience is required.

Comment 2: Vorlesung Mo 10-12 und Mi 10-12 im ETA F 5 mit Videoübertragung ins ETF E 1.
Short Description: Stress tensor, distortions, linear elastic bodies, special bending of prismatic beams, numerical methods, more general bending problems, torsion, work and deformation energy, energy theorems and methods, buckling.
